executive_summary: Vision-language-action and world models for manipulation continue to mature, with new frameworks targeting mobile manipulation (SG-VLA), physics-consistent video world models (ABot-PhysWorld), and tactile-augmented policies (VTAM).

Equivariant and diffusion-based control policies are being pushed toward higher efficiency and robustness for manipulation (E3Flow, DiSCo), including edge-deployable VLA variants for industrial pose rectification (Agile-VLA).

New dexterous and soft-manipulation hardware and models span underactuated hands (PHANTOM Hand), continuum and soft manipulators (caSSM, DER-based trajectory generation), and aerial continuum manipulators with coupled dynamics and visual servoing.

Hybrid and hierarchical control architectures appear across domains: TO+RL for free-flying multi-arm space robots, decomposition of grinding into geometric planning plus local force adaptation, and multi-agent RL for collaborative carrying with coupled quadrupeds.

Safety, benchmarking, and shared autonomy are emphasized via stoppability monitors for humanoids, CaP-X for code-as-policy manipulation agents, and diffusion-based copilots that correct user inputs in high-DOF tasks.

Institution-aligned work features ETH Zurich on coupled quadruped carrying and continuum control, Stanford co-authoring actuator-aware SSMs, and CMU advancing active mobile perception in agriculture and large-scale forest/invasive-species mapping.

institution_matches.title: Learning Multi-Agent Local Collision-Avoidance for Collaborative Carrying tasks with Coupled Quadrupedal Robots
institution_matches.overview: Two quadrupeds mechanically coupled to a carried object are controlled via a hierarchical RL policy that tracks commanded velocity while avoiding obstacles using only onboard sensing. The method removes dependence on maps and precomputed paths, outperforming optimization-based and decentralized RL baselines in unknown environments.

institution_matches.title: Learning Actuator-Aware Spectral Submanifolds for Precise Control of Continuum Robots
institution_matches.overview: Control-augmented spectral submanifolds explicitly embed actuator inputs into reduced-order models of continuum robots, capturing nonlinear state–input couplings without separate calibration. On a tendon-driven trunk, the approach reduces prediction error by 40% and closed-loop tracking error by 52% versus Koopman and prior SSM controllers while running in real time.

institution_matches.title: Active Robotic Perception for Disease Detection and Mapping in Apple Trees
institution_matches.overview: An autonomous ground robot performs active perception for fire-blight detection in orchards using flash stereo RGB, real-time depth, and semantic 3D mapping with confidence tracking. Next-best-view planners—volumetric and semantic—significantly improve coverage and detection F1 over a geometric baseline in lab and simulated tree studies.

institution_matches.title: Allometric Scaling Laws for Bipedal Robots
institution_matches.overview: By surveying existing legged robots and running controlled simulations, the authors derive empirical scaling laws for mass, speed, and torque over three orders of magnitude in leg length. Robot mass is found to scale closer to L² and required torque to mL, differing significantly from isometric and biological expectations, with implications for size-aware design.

institution_matches.title: Planning over MAPF Agent Dependencies via Multi-Dependency PIBT
institution_matches.overview: MD-PIBT generalizes Priority Inheritance with Backtracking by explicitly planning over agent dependencies, recovering PIBT/EPIBT as special cases. Configured appropriately, it scales to 10,000 agents with various kinodynamic constraints and improves performance, especially for large agents in dense multi-agent path-finding scenarios.

institution_matches.title: MapForest: A Modular Field Robotics System for Forest Mapping and Invasive Species Localization
institution_matches.overview: MapForest provides a platform-agnostic LiDAR-inertial-RGB payload and software stack for GNSS-challenged forest environments, fusing mapping with image-based invasive-species detection. Field trials show sub-2 m drift over 1.2 km and reliable Tree-of-Heaven localization, yielding GIS-ready maps for ecological management.

institution_matches.title: Design Guidelines for Nonlinear Kalman Filters via Covariance Compensation
institution_matches.overview: The paper introduces covariance compensation as a lens for understanding nonlinear Kalman filter behavior, measuring deviation from EKF covariance. From this, three design guidelines—orthogonal invariance, sufficient compensation beyond EKF, and favoring underconfidence—are derived and empirically shown to improve estimation over common fixed-parameter choices.

topic_matches.title: SG-VLA: Learning Spatially-Grounded Vision-Language-Action Models for Mobile Manipulation
topic_matches.overview: SG-VLA targets household mobile manipulation with a 13D action space for base, arm, and gripper by enriching inputs (multi-view RGB-D and short histories) and co-training auxiliary decoders for poses, grasps, and segmentation. The auxiliary spatial tasks yield strongly grounded latent representations that significantly improve performance on pick-place and door-like operations over direct imitation learning.

topic_matches.title: Efficient Hybrid SE(3)-Equivariant Visuomotor Flow Policy via Spherical Harmonics for Robot Manipulation
topic_matches.overview: E3Flow is a rectified-flow-based visuomotor policy that enforces SO(3) equivariance via spherical harmonics and fuses RGB and point clouds through an invariant feature enhancement module. Across 8 MimicGen tasks and real robots, it modestly surpasses Spherical Diffusion Policy in success rate while achieving a 7× inference speedup, improving the performance–efficiency trade-off in equivariant manipulation policies.

topic_matches.title: PHANTOM Hand
topic_matches.overview: The PHANTOM hand is a 6-actuator, 15-DoF tendon-driven, underactuated hand that couples analytic kinematic modeling with compliant grasping. A sparse geometry-based mapping and mechanics-based compensation suppress kinematic drift, enabling sub-degree repeatability for free-motion shaping while preserving robust, predictable force output across diverse grasps.

topic_matches.title: Grounding Sim-to-Real Generalization in Dexterous Manipulation: An Empirical Study with Vision-Language-Action Models
topic_matches.overview: Through 10k+ real trials, the authors systematically evaluate how domain randomization, photorealistic rendering, accurate physics, and RL updates affect sim-to-real transfer of VLA-based dexterous manipulation policies. They release hardware platforms and a detailed protocol that varies backgrounds, lighting, distractors, and object sets, providing a realistic benchmark for generalist dexterous manipulation.

topic_matches.title: Agile-VLA: Few-Shot Industrial Pose Rectification via Implicit Affordance Anchoring
topic_matches.overview: Agile-VLA decouples slow semantic inference from high-rate control on edge devices via implicit affordance anchoring that maps geometric cues to parametric action primitives. An asynchronous dual-stream design (10 Hz perception, 50 Hz control) enables robust few-shot (5 demos) pose rectification of irregular industrial workpieces on a 6-DoF arm running on Jetson-class hardware.

topic_matches.title: CaP-X: A Framework for Benchmarking and Improving Coding Agents for Robot Manipulation
topic_matches.overview: CaP-X introduces CaP-Gym and CaP-Bench to study code-as-policy agents that control robots by writing executable programs over perception and control primitives. Evaluations of 12 LLM/VLMs show dependence on human-designed abstractions, but also that richer agentic strategies (multi-turn feedback, visual differencing, automatic skill synthesis) can recover near-human reliability and enable RL-based sim-to-real transfer.

topic_matches.title: VTAM: Video-Tactile-Action Models for Complex Physical Interaction Beyond VLAs
topic_matches.overview: VTAM augments video-based action models with tactile streams through lightweight cross-modal finetuning and a tactile-regularized attention objective. On contact-rich manipulation, including fragile potato-chip pick-and-place, it sustains ~90% success and exceeds a pi 0.5 baseline by 80% in particularly force-sensitive tasks, highlighting the value of tactile grounding for foundation action models.

topic_matches.title: Strain-Parameterized Coupled Dynamics and Dual-Camera Visual Servoing for Aerial Continuum Manipulators
topic_matches.overview: The paper unifies Cosserat-rod strain-based modeling of a tendon-driven continuum arm with UAV rigid-body dynamics into an SE(3) Lagrangian ODE, explicitly accounting for the underactuated aerial base. A dual-camera IBVS controller with adaptive low-level compensation mitigates limited FoV and attitude-induced image motion, validated on a custom aerial-continuum prototype in simulation and experiments.

topic_matches.title: Path Planning and Reinforcement Learning-Driven Control of On-Orbit Free-Flying Multi-Arm Robots
topic_matches.overview: A hybrid framework uses trajectory optimization to generate dynamically feasible motions for thruster-equipped free-flying multi-arm robots and RL to robustly track them under disturbances. Simulated case studies—initial contact and fully free-floating surface interactions—show smoother, safer, and more efficient maneuvers than traditional strategies, with RL effectively compensating model mismatches.

topic_matches.title: Task-Aware Positioning for Improvisational Tasks in Mobile Construction Robots via an AI Agent with Multi-LMM Modules
topic_matches.overview: A quadruped-based construction agent decomposes improvisational natural-language tasks into three parallel LMM modules for task parsing, drawing-based navigation, and visual reasoning about non-predefined locations. The system achieves over 92% success in identifying and positioning at task-required locations, advancing task-aware base placement for mobile construction robots.
